iterations.title: Map the Repository seam and choose the second backing
Resolve the forks and plan the backing-swap slices
E1: TrackingStore protocol + FilesystemStore; Repository delegates its own I/O
E2: relocate Workstream persistence behind the store; fix cli leaks + 2 CLI bugs
E3: SQLiteStore second backing + LUME_BACKING selection; lifecycle exercised against it
E4: InMemoryStore double + cross-backing contract test matrix
E5: closeout - retro, naming decision, lume close

plan.sketch: E1: Define the TrackingStore protocol + FilesystemStore (wraps today's path logic: list/has/create + read/write per artifact). Repository takes an injected store and routes its OWN I/O (state.json, objective.json, .lume/ listing) through it. Behaviour-preserving; fs default; suite green. DONE = grep shows Repository does no direct path read/write; tests pass.

E2: Relocate Workstream persistence behind the store. Workstream takes (store, slug) instead of a Path; objective/iteration-content/decisions/retro/state reads+writes go through the store. Fix the cli path-printing + the retro verb's direct ws._path read (F7). ALSO fix two CLI bugs found in planning: (a) `lume plan add` ignores the -g/tag flag its usage advertises - wire -g so optional items can be tagged from the CLI; (b) `lume get plan` errors because the entity is 'plan_item' - accept 'plan' as an alias (or document the real name). DONE = no filesystem access outside FilesystemStore (+ migrate.py); plan add -g and get plan both work; suite green.

E3: Second backing - SQLiteStore implementing TrackingStore ((slug,artifact)->row). Selection via LUME_BACKING env in cli.main (fs default), constructor-injected. Run the full lifecycle (new/open/transitions/decide/retro/close) against SQLite in tests. DONE = replaceability demonstrated; fs behaviour unchanged; suite green.
E4: In-memory store as a test double + a shared contract test-suite run against all backings (fs, sqlite, memory) to pin the contract. DONE = one parametrized suite green across backings.
E5: Closeout - retro (did the second backing prove replaceability for the cost?), assess done-when clause by clause, finalize the Repository/TrackingStore naming decision, lume close.
